info.name: Movate — Core Flow
info.description:
A top-to-bottom live demo of the mdk (`movate-cli`) runtime `/api/v1` surface against a deployed Azure Container Apps runtime.

Each request maps to an `mdk <verb>` and demonstrates one core feature: create (init/add), validate, KB, skills, deploy (publish), run, and monitor. Run the folder in order — `test` scripts capture `agent_name` and `run_id` into collection/environment variables so the chain flows without manual copy/paste.

**Auth:** collection-level Bearer using `{{bearer_token}}` (set it in the environment — never hardcoded). The token needs `admin` + `read` (and `run` + `kb:write` for the run/KB steps). See `postman/README.md` for how to mint one with `mdk auth create-key`.

**Source of truth:** request bodies and scopes mirror `src/movate/runtime/app.py` + `src/movate/runtime/schemas.py` and the audit in `docs/front-end-api.md`. You can also `Import → Link → {{runtime_url}}/api/v1/openapi.json` for the full typed API.

item.name: Movate — Core Flow
item.description: The ordered demo. Run from top to bottom (Postman Runner or one-by-one).

request.description: The `mdk auth whoami` equivalent. Returns the calling key's `key_id`, `tenant_id`, `env`, `scopes`, and `expires_at`. Use it to confirm the token is valid and carries `admin` + `read` (the demo also exercises `run` and `kb:write`) before running the rest of the flow. No scope gate — only authentication is required.

item.name: 1a. Create agent (from wizard JSON) — mdk init

body.raw:
{
  "name": "{{agent_name}}",
  "agent_provider": "Movate",
  "agent_type": "Task Agent",
  "role": "Assistant",
  "description": "An FAQ assistant. Answers questions concisely with a confidence score.",
  "agent_role": "Concise, friendly support agent; cites sources when grounded.",
  "agent_goal": "Answer product FAQs accurately and briefly.",
  "agent_prompt": "You are an FAQ assistant. Answer the user's question concisely using the provided knowledge base context. If you are unsure, say so.\n\nQuestion: {{question}}",
  "reference_output": "Our standard plan includes 5 seats and 100GB storage.",
  "mcp_connectors": ["web-search"],
  "knowledge_store": ["product-docs"],
  "ai_model": "openai/gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18",
  "ai_foundation": "Azure"
}

request.description:
The `mdk init` (agent-authoring half) equivalent — create an agent in the runtime registry from the Mova iO "Onboard Agent" wizard JSON (`WizardAgentSubmission`). This is the simplest live create path: structured fields → canonical `agent.yaml` + `prompt.md`, no file upload.

`mcp_connectors` → the agent's `skills` and `knowledge_store` → the agent's `contexts`, so this single request demonstrates skills + contexts wiring. **Both must already exist on the runtime** or the bundle 422s on `load_agent()`: register the skill first via request `4` (`POST /skills`) and ensure the named context exists (or remove those two arrays for a no-dependency create). Scope: `admin`. Returns `AgentCreatedView` (`name`, `version`, `agent_dir`, ...).

Note on the CLI caveat: `mdk init` on disk *also* scaffolds a local project — that filesystem half is CLI/ops-only. This endpoint is the runtime "make this agent exist on the server" half.

exec: pm.test('201 Created (or 409 if it already exists)', () => [201, 409].includes(pm.response.code));

formdata.description: OPTION A — a single .zip of the canonical layout (agent.yaml, prompt.md, schema/, optionally skills/<name>/, contexts/<name>.md, kb/). Mutually exclusive with the individual-file fields below; supply EITHER bundle OR the four files, not both.
OPTION B — agent.yaml (required in individual-files mode).
OPTION B — prompt.md (required in individual-files mode).
OPTION B — schema/input.json (required in individual-files mode).
OPTION B — schema/output.json (required in individual-files mode).
OPTIONAL, repeating — each is a contexts/<name>.md the agent declares in `contexts:`.
OPTIONAL, repeating — each is a kb/<name>.json corpus file.

request.description:
The bundle/multipart variant of the same `mdk init` / `mdk deploy --mode agents` create — `POST /api/v1/agents`. Use this when you already have a pre-built canonical bundle (e.g. produced by `mdk init --llm` locally, since there is NO server-side LLM authoring endpoint — see `docs/front-end-api.md`).

Two mutually-exclusive modes: attach a single `bundle` .zip (Option A, enabled below), OR enable the four individual files `agent_yaml` + `prompt` + `input_schema` + `output_schema` (Option B). A bundle that ships `skills/<name>/` folders persists those skills into the registry first, then the agent — so the full skill + context wiring can ride in one upload. Scope: `admin`. Returns `AgentCreatedView`.

This request is illustrative — attach your own file(s) before sending. Disabled by default in the auto-run is the individual-file set; pick one mode.

The `mdk validate` equivalent — run the prompt linter + cost forecast against the already-stored agent. No request body. Returns `AgentValidationView` (`passed`, `errors[]`, `warnings[]`, `cost_forecast`). This is the soft "is it shippable?" gate; the hard structural validation already ran at create time (a malformed bundle 422s before it ever lands). Scope: `read` (read-only despite being a POST).

formdata.description: One or more documents to ingest. Repeat the `files` key for multiple uploads. Supported: .md / .markdown / .txt / .pdf / .docx / .html / .htm. Attach a small doc (e.g. a product-FAQ.md) before sending.

request.description:
The `mdk kb ingest` equivalent — upload one or more documents into the agent's knowledge base. `multipart/form-data` with a repeating `files` field; each doc is chunked, embedded, and persisted (deduped on content hash). Returns `KbIngestView` (`files[]` with per-file `status`, `total_chunks_saved`). Scope: `kb:write`.

Note: this endpoint takes uploaded files only — there is no inline-text or URL body field. To ingest a URL, fetch it to an .html/.md file first and upload that. Attach your own small doc before sending (this request ships no file).

The `mdk kb stats` equivalent — server-side aggregate of the agent's KB. Returns `KbStatsView` (`total_chunks`, `total_chars`, `ocr_chunks`, per-`sources` breakdown, distinct embedding `models`). Confirms the ingest from 3a actually landed. Scope: `read`.

The `mdk kb search` equivalent — semantic search over the agent's KB (`KbSearchSubmission`: `question`, `k`, `hybrid`). Set `hybrid: true` to fuse vector + BM25 lexical results. Returns `KbSearchView` with scored `results[]`. Shows the retrieval that grounds the run in step 6. Scope: `read`.

formdata.description: REQUIRED — the skill.yaml spec. Its `name:` field decides the on-disk skill directory and is the name an agent references in `skills: [...]`.
OPTIONAL — corpus.json shipped alongside the skill.

request.description:
The `mdk skills add` (skill-registration) equivalent — create or replace a skill bundle in the runtime's skill registry (PUT semantics: re-uploading the same name overwrites). `multipart/form-data`: `skill_yaml` (required) + optional `impl` / `corpus` / `readme`. Returns `SkillCreatedView`. Scope: `admin`.

Run this BEFORE request 1a if your wizard body references this skill in `mcp_connectors` — an agent declaring `skills: [<name>]` 422s if the skill isn't already registered. Attach your own skill.yaml before sending.

The agent-level `mdk deploy` (publish/promote) equivalent — push the agent's canonical bundle to the per-tenant GitHub repo as one commit (ADR 007). `AgentPublishSubmission`: all fields optional (`commit_message`, `author_name`, `author_email`). Returns `AgentPublishedView` (commit SHA + URL). Scope: `admin`.

Requires `MDK_GITHUB_ENABLED=1` + GitHub App config on the runtime, else 503 (the route is still advertised). CLI caveat: this is the *agent promotion* sense of deploy — the *infra* sense (build the runtime image, roll Azure Container Apps) stays CLI/ops-only via `mdk deploy --mode runtime`.

exec: pm.test('200, or 503 when GitHub publishing is disabled on the runtime', () => [200, 503].includes(pm.response.code));
else { console.log('Publish disabled (MDK_GITHUB_ENABLED off) — expected in many demo runtimes.'); }

query.description: Inline (synchronous) mode — execute in-process and return the RunView (200). Omit or set false to queue an async job (202 + job_id) and poll GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}.

request.description:
The `mdk run` equivalent — submit an agent run. `AgentRunSubmission`: `input` (matches the agent's input schema — here `{question}`), optional `mock`, `notify_email`, `thread_id`. With `?wait=true` the runtime executes synchronously and returns a `RunView` with the grounded answer (200). Default (`wait=false`) queues a job and returns 202 + `{job_id}`. Scope: `run`.

The `test` script captures `run_id` into a collection/environment variable so the monitor steps (7a/7b) can fetch it. Tip: add `"mock": true` to the body to run against the deterministic MockProvider (no API key, sub-second) for a guaranteed-fast demo.

item.name: 7a. Monitor — get run — mdk runs get

request.description: The `mdk runs get` equivalent — fetch a single run including its `output`, status, timing, and cost. Returns `RunView`. Uses the `{{run_id}}` captured in step 6. Scope: `read`.

item.name: 7b. Monitor — run trace — mdk runs trace

request.description: The trace-viewer / `mdk runs trace` equivalent — the reconstructed span tree for the run (prompt, model calls, tool/skill invocations, retrieval). Returns `RunTraceView`. This is the observability payoff of the demo. Scope: `read`.

query.description: OPTIONAL — only count runs / evals from the last N days. 0 (default) = all-time. Mirrors `mdk report --last N`. Range 0–3650.
OPTIONAL — how many failing cases to surface in `top_failing_cases` (default 5). Range 1–50.

request.description:
The `mdk report` equivalent — the cross-agent aggregate monitor feed (ADR 032 D2). Returns `ReportView`: tenant-scoped pass-rate trends, cost-over-time, latency p50/p95/p99, top failing cases, and a per-agent/workflow rollup over the requested `window`. This is the fleet-wide "how are my agents doing?" panel the Mova iO front end renders — the aggregate complement to the per-run fetch + trace (7a/7b). Tenant-scoped; an empty store returns a zeroed report (200), never a 500. Scope: `read`.

Optional query params (disabled by default — enable to scope the rollup): `?window=<days>` narrows to the last N days (0 = all-time) and `?top=<n>` sets how many failing cases to surface. For a single agent's slice use `GET /api/v1/agents/{name}/metrics` (same `read` scope, same params).
